units.description: Extend skills/development-harness/schemas/phase-graph.json: add required depends_on, touches_paths (required when parallel_safe: true), and parallel_safe on units; add illustrative example data
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/schemas/phase-graph.json now declares depends_on, touches_paths, parallel_safe on every unit across 2 example phases and 6 units

Example demonstrates: depends_on chain in PHASE_001 (unit_002 depends on unit_001), independent siblings in PHASE_002, parallel_safe:false without touches_paths (unit_001), parallel_safe:true with touches_paths globs (5 other units)
python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests exits 0 (36/36 tests pass)

units.description: Extend skills/development-harness/schemas/state.json: add execution.fleet block (mode, batch_id, per-unit fleet entries) with example
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/schemas/state.json now carries execution.fleet with mode ('idle'/'dispatched'/'merging'), batch_id, and units[]

Per-unit fleet entries carry unit_id, phase_id, worktree_path, branch, status ('running'/'succeeded'/'failed'/'merged'), started_at, ended_at, agent_summary_path, and conflict (null or {paths, strategy_applied})
Example demonstrates a dispatched batch with three units: one succeeded, one running, one failed with conflict metadata

units.description: Extend skills/development-harness/schemas/config.json: add execution_mode.parallelism, execution_mode.agent_delegation, execution_mode.versioning blocks
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/schemas/config.json: execution_mode promoted from string to object with location + three sub-blocks
parallelism: enabled=false, max_concurrent_units=3, conflict_strategy=abort_batch, require_touches_paths=true, allow_cross_phase=false
versioning: break_on_schema_bump=true (default matches the clean-code direction chosen for this project)

units.description: Register .harness/worktrees/ and .harness/logs/ in skills/development-harness/schemas/manifest.json and add entries to the .gitignore template
Bump SCHEMA_VERSION from 1.0 to 2.0 in skills/development-harness/scripts/harness_utils.py; check_schema_version emits actionable re-create error on mismatch

Extend skills/development-harness/scripts/validate_harness.py: required-field enforcement (no inference), cycle detection for depends_on, touches_paths path-safety checks, version gate with actionable error; add tests including v1 fixture rejection

Rewrite skills/development-harness/scripts/select_next_unit.py around frontier computation; add --frontier and --max N flags; no legacy list-order fallback; preserve stop-hook JSON contract on no-flag call
Implement frontier resolution strictly from depends_on; error loudly on malformed units; cover linear, diamond, disconnected, partially-completed graphs
New skills/development-harness/scripts/compute_parallel_batch.py with stdlib fnmatch glob-overlap matrix; greedy-pack under max_concurrent_units; emits {batch, excluded, batch_id}
Machine-readable exclusion reasons ('not_parallel_safe', 'path_overlap_with:<unit_id>', 'capacity_cap'); unit tests asserting each reason fires on a crafted input
Insert Exploration step in skills/development-harness/commands/invoke.md; dispatch Agent(Explore) for refactor/extend/fix/migrate/update keywords before implementation
Mirror Exploration step in skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md
Add multi-file parallel-edit guidance (fan out to 2-3 Agent calls in one message when >=4 independent files) to both invoke command docs
Add parallel phase-review dispatch guidance (run code-review skill concurrently with commit-agent-changes) in both invoke command docs
Update skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/rules/harness-core.md and the Cursor mirror with delegate-vs-inline guidance

Rewrite validation step in skills/development-harness/commands/invoke.md to fan out Layer 1 (lint + typecheck + formatter) and Layer 2 (unit tests) as concurrent Bash calls when config.agent_delegation.parallel_validation_layers == true
Mirror parallel-validation-layer step in skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md
Update evidence-recording format in both invoke command docs to include per-layer timing (e.g., 'pnpm lint exits 0 (2.1s)')

New skills/development-harness/scripts/dispatch_batch.py: creates git worktrees under .harness/worktrees/<batch_id>/<unit_id>, creates harness/<batch_id>/<unit_id> branches, seeds WORKTREE_UNIT.json, writes state.execution.fleet; atomic teardown on failure

New skills/development-harness/scripts/merge_batch.py: serial fan-in via git merge --no-ff; conflict_strategy=abort_batch or serialize_conflicted; post-merge validation; git reset --hard rollback on failure
New skills/development-harness/scripts/teardown_batch.py: idempotent cleanup of worktrees and harness/batch_* branches; safe to run under /clear, /sync, and error recovery
Scope-violation detector in merge_batch.py: computes git diff --name-only <merge-base>..<branch> and rejects any unit whose diff exceeds declared touches_paths, regardless of sub-agent self-report
Add .harness/.lock file-based mutex around merge_batch.py with O_EXCL; contention test verifies serialization
Extend skills/development-harness/scripts/sync_harness.py to detect orphaned worktrees, stale fleet entries, and harness/batch_* branches lacking fleet entries
Integration test skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/integration/test_parallel_invoke.py: fixture git repo with 3-unit phase, shell-scripted fake agents, asserts final state and cleanup

New skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/agents/harness-unit.md: system prompt, tool allowlist (no git push; no writes to .harness/; no writes outside worktree), required JSON report schema
Update skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/rules/harness-core.md with orchestrator-vs-sub-agent rules (only orchestrator merges; sub-agents commit only in worktree; never push/rebase)
Mirror orchestrator-vs-sub-agent rules in skills/development-harness/templates/rules/harness-core.mdc for Cursor

Rewrite skills/development-harness/commands/invoke.md into a single batch-driven flow: batch of 1 runs in-tree, batch of N fans out via Agent(harness-unit); same dispatch/merge/validate/commit checkpoints
Mirror the single-flow rewrite in skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md
Document one-turn-per-batch semantics in skills/development-harness/references/architecture.md (session_count increments once per turn)
Wire parallel phase-review dispatch (code-review skill concurrent with commit-agent-changes) into the rewritten flow
End-to-end integration test: 3-unit fixture phase with parallelism.enabled=true completes with one merge; batch-of-1 test produces identical final state to a worktree batch of 1
Update skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/hooks/continue-loop.py: pre-check that stops when state.execution.fleet.mode != 'idle'; deletes .invoke-active on stop
Mirror fleet-mode guard in skills/development-harness/templates/hooks/continue-loop.py (Cursor variant)
Unit tests for both hooks simulating fleet.mode = 'dispatched' (stop) and 'idle' (existing behavior); manual verification checklist for mid-batch kill
Session-scoped kill switch: write .harness/.parallel-disabled after 2 scope_violation or ambiguity failures in a session; cleared when .invoke-active clears
Verify batch-of-1 uses the same in-tree path as parallelism=false; re-assert via test that no special-case branch exists in invoke command docs
Document scope-violation always-on policy in skills/development-harness/references/phase-contract.md
Concurrent merge serialization test: two merge_batch.py invocations contend on .harness/.lock; second waits for first
Extend skills/development-harness/templates/checkpoint-template.md with a Batch section (batch ID, mode, per-unit status and branch, conflicts summary)
Update skills/development-harness/commands/state.md and templates/workspace-commands/harness-state.md to render fleet status, orphaned worktrees, and per-batch timings
Log directory creation logic for .harness/logs/<batch_id>/ with batch.json, <unit_id>.md, merge.log, validation.log; logs are harness-owned and gitignored
Integration test running a sample batch asserts all log artifacts are produced and /harness-state renders fleet + log pointers

Add 'Parallel Execution Model' section to skills/development-harness/references/architecture.md: worktree-per-unit, orchestrator/sub-agent boundary, frontier+overlap check, dispatch-wait-merge lifecycle, conflict strategies, when to enable/not

Update skills/development-harness/references/phase-contract.md to document required depends_on, touches_paths (when parallel_safe: true), and parallel_safe; add 'Decomposing a phase for parallelism' subsection
New skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md: dispatch lifecycle, overlap-matrix algorithm, merge-order rationale, failure-mode catalog, recovery procedures, readiness checklist

Update skills/development-harness/commands/create.md Phase 2 with two structured questions: (1) Enable parallel unit execution? (y/n, default n) -> config.execution_mode.parallelism.enabled; (2) Break-on-schema-bump vs migrate? (break/migrate, default break) -> config.execution_mode.versioning.break_on_schema_bump. Update Phase 4 to instruct proposing touches_paths per unit and setting parallel_safe: false when blast radius is unknown

Update skills/development-harness/SKILL.md with 'Parallel execution (optional)' subsection and 'Version upgrades' note: re-run /create-development-harness on schema_version changes; no migration script provided
Cross-check pass: every new field, script, rule, and command introduced by this roadmap is referenced in at least one doc under skills/development-harness/references/

Add parallelism readiness checklist to skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md: touches_paths on every parallelism-eligible unit, no shared-aggregator units, CI handles multi-commit pushes
Add 'Version upgrades' note to skills/development-harness/SKILL.md: re-run /create-development-harness when schema_version changes; no migration script is provided by design

Update skills/development-harness/commands/update.md (and the workspace template) to detect schema_version mismatch between installed harness and skill; emit a pointer to the recreate flow; test coverage via a v1 fixture

Create fixture at skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/fixtures/self-test/: throwaway workspace with a 2-phase / 6-unit mini-CRUD roadmap designed to exercise a batch >= 2, one merge conflict, and one scope violation
Run /create-development-harness on the fixture; populate touches_paths on every unit; enable parallelism; capture the generated phase-graph and config for the record
Run /invoke-development-harness iteratively until all fixture phases complete; ensure the run exercises batch >= 2, one merge conflict, and one scope violation
Capture execution traces; produce a data table comparing wall-clock with parallelism enabled vs a sequential baseline; verify no orphaned worktrees/branches after completion
Write post-mortem under skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/fixtures/self-test/POST-MORTEM.md and link it from skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md
